title: Marathon Digital Holdings' Operational Efficiency Gaps: A Drag on Performance in a High-Cost Bitcoin Mining Landscape
text: Marathon Digital Holdings reported a bitcoin yield per share of 62.9% for 2024, showcasing operational efficiency and profitability. The company maintained a competitive direct energy cost of $28.8K per Bitcoin from its owned sites in 2024. In Q1 2024, Marathon reported an average cost of electricity of 4.7 cents per kilowatt-hour. Fleet efficiency improvements showed continuous progress in energy consumption metrics including 23.1 J/TH by end of 2023. For full year 2024, Marathon mined 9,457 BTC and held 44,893 BTC valued at $4.2 billion at year-end. These metrics demonstrate Marathon Digital's focus on vertical integration and securing low-cost energy sources to maintain competitive advantages in bitcoin mining.
